title: Government response: Bringing goods into the UK temporarily
body: "The government will take forward a set of targeted simplifications and improvements to the Temporary Admission (TA) procedure. TA allows goods to be imported into the UK temporarily without payment of customs duty and import VAT, subject to certain rules and requirements.  This response sets out the detail of our improvements, which cover 3 areas:    extending and simplifying timing requirements improving user experience and guidance removing specific restrictions on who is eligible to use TA and how certain goods can be used while under TA    The changes set out in this response are the outcome of further engagement with industry to explore options for improvements to TA. HM Treasury and HM Revenue and Customs committed to this in the summary of responses to the Call for Evidence on TA.    These simplifications and improvements will support businesses whilst protecting our people and domestic markets. They will also improve the customer experience for businesses engaging with customs. They will be implemented during 2025.  "

taxons.title: Customs declarations, duties and tariffs (import and export)
